title: ATAH Query — Component 1 (Discovery)
description: ATAH Registry Operation Profile object: Discovery query body for POST /v1/query and the `find_professional` MCP tool input (spec/v0.8 Section 4.11). Component 1 is the foundation of the protocol; Component 2 (Consumer-self handoff) is gated by `request_intent: 'self'`. Required for implementations claiming Registry Conformance.

request_intent.description: Gates which next-step components are available. `self`: requesting AI is acting for someone seeking a professional for themselves; Component 2 (Consumer-self handoff) is available next. `on_behalf_of_client`: requesting AI is a professional's AI assistant identifying candidates for a client referral; Component 2 is NOT available — the professional handles the introduction off-platform under their own consent capture with the client.

limit.description: Maximum number of candidates to return. Required. The upper bound of 100 reflects the protocol's posture that Discovery returns a working candidate set, not an exhaustive directory; AI agents requesting larger sets should narrow `matter` filters or paginate via category-aware refinement rather than expanding `limit`.

consent_receipt_id.description: Identifier for the consent receipt covering this Discovery query. Currently required for all Discovery queries at v0.8.2. Conditional requirements by `request_intent` are targeted for v0.8.3 (see ROADMAP); under those conditional requirements, the precise scope of the `consent_receipt_id` requirement may vary by intent context.

ordering_preference.description: Optional explicit ordering preference. When omitted, the response is ordered by stratified randomisation per spec §9 (default ordering policy). When supplied, ATAH applies the named mode and records the choice on the response's `presentation_disclosure.ordering_policy.mode`. Normative rule: ATAH may determine eligibility and exclusion, but MUST NOT express preference among eligible candidates unless the ordering basis is explicit, non-commercial, and disclosed. The explicit modes here satisfy that disclosure requirement.

parameter.description: Required for modes that need an accompanying value (`specific_language` → an ISO 639-1 code such as `en`; `specific_category_qualification` → a qualification identifier; `professional_type` / `jurisdiction` → the relevant identifier). Absent for modes that don't need a parameter (`nearest`, `soonest_available`, `remote_available`, `highest_verification_confidence`).
